Women, Infants and Children (WIC)
Women, Infants and Children (WIC)
En Español
Welcome to the County of San Luis Obispo WIC Program
Women, Infants and Children (
WIC
) Program helps families by providing nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy foods, and referrals to health care and other community services.
Call (805) 781-5570 or text (888) 417-6180
and someone will be with you shortly!
New!
WIC is on the go - coming to a place near you.
See our pop-up clinic schedule.
What is the process?
Check Eligibility
To find out if you are eligible, view WIC’s
qualifying incomes
, visit the
State's WIC website
or fill out the
Apply for WIC
form.
Make an Appointment
Please call (805) 781-5570 or text (888) 417-6180 to schedule an appointment.
Gather Necessary Documents
Identification for the persons applying for WIC, such as a birth certificate, driver’s license, crib card, military ID, photo ID, Social Security card, hospital record, or Medi-Cal card
Proof of all family income (pay stubs or Medi-Cal card)
Proof of street address (mail, utility bill, rent/mortgage receipt)
Who is eligible?
Individuals who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or just had a baby in the past 6 months (or pregnancy loss);
Children up to 5 years of age, including those cared for by a mom, dad, a grandparent, foster parent, step-parent or guardian; and
Meet the
WIC income guidelines
or who receive Medi-Cal, CalWORKS or CalFresh (SNAP) benefits; and
Live in California.
Take the online
WIC Eligibility Assessment
to see if you may qualify or fill out the
Apply for WIC
form.
When and where is this service offered?
Please contact the
WIC office
nearest you to schedule an appointment, text toll free (888) 417-6180 or fill out the
Apply for WIC
form.

Click on location name to show hours of operation, directions and phone information

FAQs
▾
Am I eligible?
To find out if you are eligible, view WIC’s
qualifying incomes
or visit
the state's WIC website
.
▾
Are appointments required?
Yes.  In order to enroll in WIC, an appointment is
required
.  Please call (805) 781-5570 or text (888) 417-6180 to schedule an appointment.
▾
What food benefits do WIC participants receive?
WIC participants receive monthly food benefits to get healthy foods.  WIC foods include fruits and vegetables, whole-wheat bread, dry beans/peas, peanut butter, milk, soymilk and tofu, yogurt, eggs, cheese, cereal, canned fish, baby foods and infant cereal. Special infant formulas and medical foods may be provided when prescribed by a physician for a specified medical condition.  For a full list of eligible foods, click
here
.
▾
Will I receive WIC benefits at my first appointment?
Yes!  WIC benefits will be provided on a California WIC Card for all of your family's food benefits at your first appointment if you qualify.
▾
Where can I find more information?
For more information on the program and its many benefits, visit
the California WIC Program website
.
▾
Where can I use my WIC Card?
Only a
WIC authorized food store
can accept the WIC Card. Look for the WIC logo or WIC poster to identify stores that accept the WIC Card. You can also consult with your WIC counselor to find a store near you.
▾
Can WIC be used at Farmers’ Markets?
Yes, new WIC cards (summer 2025) with QR codes can be used year-round at authorized Farmers' Markets to purchase fresh fruits and vegetables throughout San Luis Obispo County!
Penny Borenstein, MD, MPH
Health Officer/Public Health Director
Director of Health Agency | Public Health: Penny Borenstein, MD, MPH, Health Officer/Public Health Director
